title: Bertiera pubiflora
taxonomy: Bertiera pubiflora is a species of plant in the family Rubiaceae. It belongs to the genus Bertiera, which is a group of flowering plants native to tropical Africa. The plant's classification is further broken down into its kingdom, with Bertiera pubiflora being classified as a member of the kingdom Plantae. This classification also places Bertiera pubiflora within the clade Angiosperms, which includes all flowering plants. Bertiera pubiflora's classification also reflects its relationship to other plants in the family Rubiaceae, including the coffee and gardenia plants.
uses: Bertiera pubiflora is utilized in traditional medicine, primarily in the treatment of various ailments. It has been traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory conditions. The plant's extracts are believed to possess antimicrobial and antipyretic properties. Bertiera pubiflora is also used to treat gastrointestinal issues, such as diarrhea and dysentery.
